test_cases.description: NewAdminCmd returns a *cobra.Command with Use='admin', no RunE, and HasSubCommands() true.
test_cases.input: call NewAdminCmd()
test_cases.expected: returned command has Use == 'admin', RunE == nil, HasSubCommands() == true
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cmd = NewAdminCmd(); assert cmd.Use == 'admin'; assert cmd.RunE == nil; assert cmd.HasSubCommands() == true

test_cases.description: The admin users command group registers exactly the eight required subcommands: list, show, create, update, promote, demote, block, unblock.
test_cases.input: call newAdminUsersCmd()
test_cases.expected: the returned command has eight subcommands with Use values matching list, show, create, update, promote, demote, block, unblock
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cmd = newAdminUsersCmd(); names = [c.Name() for c in cmd.Commands()]; assert set(names) == {'list','show','create','update','promote','demote','block','unblock'}

test_cases.description: The admin orgs command group registers list, create, update, delete, block, unblock, and a members sub-group containing list, add, remove.
test_cases.input: call newAdminOrgsCmd()
test_cases.expected: direct children include list/create/update/delete/block/unblock/members; members children include list/add/remove
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: orgsCmd = newAdminOrgsCmd(); topNames = [c.Name() for c in orgsCmd.Commands()]; assert set(topNames) >= {'list','create','update','delete','block','unblock','members'}; membersCmd = find(orgsCmd.Commands(), name='members'); memberNames = [c.Name() for c in membersCmd.Commands()]; assert set(memberNames) == {'list','add','remove'}

test_cases.description: The admin keys command group registers exactly two subcommands: list and revoke.
test_cases.input: call newAdminKeysCmd()
test_cases.expected: returned command has exactly subcommands list and revoke
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cmd = newAdminKeysCmd(); names = [c.Name() for c in cmd.Commands()]; assert set(names) == {'list','revoke'}

test_cases.description: The admin tokens command group registers exactly two subcommands: list and revoke.
test_cases.input: call newAdminTokensCmd()
test_cases.expected: returned command has exactly subcommands list and revoke
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cmd = newAdminTokensCmd(); names = [c.Name() for c in cmd.Commands()]; assert set(names) == {'list','revoke'}

test_cases.description: NewAdminCmd is the only exported constructor in the admin CLI package; all others are unexported.
test_cases.input: inspect exported symbols in internal/cli/admin*.go files
test_cases.expected: only NewAdminCmd is exported; newAdminUsersCmd, newAdminOrgsCmd, newAdminOrgsMembersCmd, newAdminKeysCmd, newAdminTokensCmd are all unexported
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: symbols = exported_functions('internal/cli', 'admin*.go'); assert symbols == ['NewAdminCmd']

test_cases.description: Every admin subcommand with a RunE executes loadConfig, newClient, validates args/flags, calls the SDK method, and prints JSON to stdout on success.
preconditions: loadConfig and newClient stubbed to return valid config and client
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users list with valid config
test_cases.expected: loadConfig called once, newClient called once, ListUsers called once, printJSON writes valid JSON to stdout, exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock = MockClient(); capture stdout; invoke 'admin users list' with mock; assert mock.ListUsersCalled == true; assert isValidJSON(stdout); assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: When the SDK method returns *apikit.APIError, handleError is called, the JSON error envelope is printed to stdout, and exit code is 1.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users show <id> where SDK returns APIError 403
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":403,"message":"forbidden"}}, exit code is 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.GetUserByIDReturns = APIError{Code:403, Message:'forbidden'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users show user-1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.code == 403; assert parsed.error.message == 'forbidden'; assert exitCode == 1

test_cases.description: When the SDK method returns a non-APIError (e.g. network failure), handleError prints a code-0 error envelope to stdout and exit code is 2.
preconditions: mock SDK client returns errors.New('connection refused')
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users list where SDK returns plain network error
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":0,"message":"connection refused"}}, exit code is 2
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.ListUsersReturns = error('connection refused'); capture stdout; invoke 'admin users list'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.code == 0; assert 'connection refused' in parsed.error.message; assert exitCode == 2

test_cases.description: When loadConfig returns an error (missing API key), the command prints a code-0 JSON error envelope and exits with code 2 without calling the SDK.
preconditions: loadConfig is stubbed to return an error
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users list with missing API key in config
test_cases.expected: stdout contains error envelope with code 0, exit code 2, SDK method not called
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: loadConfigStub = func() { return nil, error('missing api key') }; mock.ListUsersCalled = false; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users list'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.code == 0; assert exitCode == 2; assert mock.ListUsersCalled == false

test_cases.description: All SDK method calls use context.Background() as the context argument.
preconditions: mock SDK client captures the context argument passed to it
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users list with valid config and mock SDK
test_cases.expected: the context passed to ListUsers is context.Background() (non-nil, no deadline)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock = CapturingMockClient(); invoke 'admin users list'; ctx = mock.CapturedContext; assert ctx != nil; hasDeadline, _ = ctx.Deadline(); assert hasDeadline == false

test_cases.description: No net/http client is constructed or used directly in admin*.go production files.
preconditions: source files internal/cli/admin.go, admin_users.go, admin_orgs.go, admin_keys.go, admin_tokens.go are accessible
test_cases.input: static analysis / grep of production admin source files
test_cases.expected: no import of 'net/http' appears in any internal/cli/admin*.go file
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for each file in ['admin.go','admin_users.go','admin_orgs.go','admin_keys.go','admin_tokens.go']: assert 'net/http' not in imports(file)

test_cases.description: When a required positional argument is absent, the command prints a missing-argument JSON error envelope to stdout and exits with code 2 without calling the SDK.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users show (no positional argument provided)
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":0,"message":"missing required argument: id"}}, exit code 2, SDK not called
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.GetUserByIDCalled = false; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users show' with args=[]; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.code == 0; assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required argument: id'; assert exitCode == 2; assert mock.GetUserByIDCalled == false

test_cases.description: When a required flag is absent, the command prints a missing-flag JSON error envelope to stdout and exits with code 2 without calling the SDK.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users create --email a@b.com --provider local --provider-id p1 (no --username)
test_cases.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":0,"message":"missing required flag: --username"}}, exit code 2, SDK not called
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.CreateUserCalled = false; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users create --email a@b.com --provider local --provider-id p1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.code == 0; assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required flag: --username'; assert exitCode == 2; assert mock.CreateUserCalled == false

test_cases.description: Extra positional arguments cause Cobra's ExactArgs validator to reject the command before RunE is called.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users show id1 id2 (extra arg)
test_cases.expected: Cobra prints a usage error before RunE executes; SDK is not called; exit code is non-zero
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.GetUserByIDCalled = false; invoke 'admin users show id1 id2'; assert exitCode != 0; assert mock.GetUserByIDCalled == false

test_cases.description: Non-UUID string IDs are passed through to the SDK without client-side validation.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users show 'not-a-uuid'
test_cases.expected: GetUserByID is called with id='not-a-uuid', SDK error is forwarded as exit code 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.GetUserByIDReturns = APIError{Code:404, Message:'user not found'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users show not-a-uuid'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'not-a-uuid'; assert exitCode == 1

test_cases.description: An empty string value for a required string flag is passed through to the SDK without client-side rejection.
preconditions: mock SDK client returns APIError{Code:400} for empty username
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users create --username '' --email a@b.com --provider local --provider-id p1
test_cases.expected: CreateUser is called with Username='', SDK error forwarded as exit code 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.CreateUserReturns = APIError{Code:400, Message:'username required'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users create --username "" --email a@b.com --provider local --provider-id p1'; assert mock.CapturedReq.Username == ''; assert exitCode == 1

test_cases.description: akc admin users list without --include-blocked calls ListUsers with IncludeBlocked:false and prints the user array as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users list (no flags)
test_cases.expected: ListUsers called with opts.IncludeBlocked==false; stdout is JSON array containing user u1; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.ListUsersReturns = [User{ID:'u1'}]; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users list'; assert mock.CapturedOpts.IncludeBlocked == false; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert len(parsed) == 1; assert parsed[0].id == 'u1'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin users list --include-blocked calls ListUsers with IncludeBlocked:true.
preconditions: mock SDK ListUsers returns []*apikit.User with a blocked user
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users list --include-blocked
test_cases.expected: ListUsers called with opts.IncludeBlocked==true; stdout is JSON array; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.ListUsersReturns = [User{ID:'u1',Blocked:true}]; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users list --include-blocked'; assert mock.CapturedOpts.IncludeBlocked == true; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin users list is registered in the agent interface with method GET, path /users, auth admin, and flag --include-blocked.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: output contains entry with name='admin users list', method='GET', path='/users', auth='admin', flags includes {name:'include-blocked', type:'bool', default:false}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: helpJSON = run('akc help --json'); cmds = JSON.parse(helpJSON).commands; entry = find(cmds, name='admin users list'); assert entry.method == 'GET'; assert entry.path == '/users'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'; flag = find(entry.flags, name='include-blocked'); assert flag.type == 'bool'; assert flag.default == false

test_cases.description: akc admin users show <id> calls GetUserByID with the given id and prints the unwrapped user as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users show u1
test_cases.expected: GetUserByID called with id='u1'; stdout is JSON user object with id='u1'; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.GetUserByIDReturns = User{ID:'u1', Username:'alice'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users show u1'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'u1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.id == 'u1'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin users show is registered in the agent interface with method GET, path /users/:id, auth admin, and positional arg id.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: output entry for 'admin users show' has method='GET', path='/users/:id', auth='admin', args=[{name:'id'}]
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: helpJSON = run('akc help --json'); entry = find(JSON.parse(helpJSON).commands, name='admin users show'); assert entry.method == 'GET'; assert entry.path == '/users/:id'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'; assert entry.args[0].name == 'id'

test_cases.description: akc admin users create with all four required flags calls CreateUser with the correct struct and prints the new user as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users create --username bob --email bob@x.com --provider local --provider-id p2
test_cases.expected: CreateUser called with {Username:'bob', Email:'bob@x.com', Provider:'local', ProviderID:'p2'}; stdout is JSON user object; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.CreateUserReturns = User{ID:'u2', Username:'bob'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users create --username bob --email bob@x.com --provider local --provider-id p2'; assert mock.CapturedReq == {Username:'bob', Email:'bob@x.com', Provider:'local', ProviderID:'p2'}; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.id == 'u2'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin users create is registered in the agent interface with method POST, path /users, auth admin, and four required string flags.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin users create' has method='POST', path='/users', auth='admin', four flags: username, email, provider, provider-id all required
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin users create'); assert entry.method == 'POST'; assert entry.path == '/users'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'; flagNames = [f.name for f in entry.flags]; assert set(flagNames) == {'username','email','provider','provider-id'}; for f in entry.flags: assert f.required == true

test_cases.description: akc admin users update <id> --full-name 'Alice B' calls UpdateUserByID with the correct id and FullName, prints user JSON.
preconditions: mock SDK UpdateUserByID returns &apikit.User{ID:'u1', FullName:'Alice B'}
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users update u1 --full-name 'Alice B'
test_cases.expected: UpdateUserByID called with id='u1', req.FullName='Alice B'; stdout is user JSON; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.UpdateUserByIDReturns = User{ID:'u1', FullName:'Alice B'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users update u1 --full-name "Alice B"'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'u1'; assert mock.CapturedReq.FullName == 'Alice B'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin users update with --full-name '' treats empty string as valid, calls SDK with FullName='' and exits 0.
preconditions: mock SDK UpdateUserByID returns a user object
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users update u1 --full-name ''
test_cases.expected: UpdateUserByID called with req.FullName=''; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.UpdateUserByIDReturns = User{ID:'u1', FullName:''}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users update u1 --full-name ""'; assert mock.CapturedReq.FullName == ''; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin users update is registered in the agent interface with method PATCH, path /users/:id, auth admin, positional arg id, required flag --full-name.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin users update' has method='PATCH', path='/users/:id', auth='admin', args=[{name:'id'}], flags=[{name:'full-name', required:true}]
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin users update'); assert entry.method == 'PATCH'; assert entry.path == '/users/:id'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'; assert entry.args[0].name == 'id'; flag = find(entry.flags, name='full-name'); assert flag.required == true

test_cases.description: akc admin users promote <id> calls PromoteUser with the given id and prints the returned user as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users promote u1
test_cases.expected: PromoteUser called with id='u1'; stdout is user JSON with role='admin'; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.PromoteUserReturns = User{ID:'u1', Role:'admin'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users promote u1'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'u1'; assert exitCode == 0; assert isValidJSON(stdout)

test_cases.description: akc admin users promote is registered in the agent interface with method POST, path /users/:id/promote, auth admin.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin users promote' has method='POST', path='/users/:id/promote', auth='admin', args=[{name:'id'}]
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin users promote'); assert entry.method == 'POST'; assert entry.path == '/users/:id/promote'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'

test_cases.description: akc admin users demote <id> calls DemoteUser with the given id and prints the returned user as JSON without performing the last-admin check.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users demote u1
test_cases.expected: DemoteUser called with id='u1'; stdout is user JSON; exit code 0; no client-side last-admin validation
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.DemoteUserReturns = User{ID:'u1', Role:'user'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users demote u1'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'u1'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin users demote is registered in the agent interface with method POST, path /users/:id/demote, auth admin.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin users demote' has method='POST', path='/users/:id/demote', auth='admin'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin users demote'); assert entry.method == 'POST'; assert entry.path == '/users/:id/demote'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'

test_cases.description: akc admin users block <id> calls BlockUser with the given id and prints the returned user as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users block u1
test_cases.expected: BlockUser called with id='u1'; stdout is user JSON; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.BlockUserReturns = User{ID:'u1', Blocked:true}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users block u1'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'u1'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin users block is registered in the agent interface with method POST, path /users/:id/block, auth admin.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin users block' has method='POST', path='/users/:id/block', auth='admin'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin users block'); assert entry.method == 'POST'; assert entry.path == '/users/:id/block'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'

test_cases.description: akc admin users unblock <id> calls UnblockUser with the given id and prints the returned user as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users unblock u1
test_cases.expected: UnblockUser called with id='u1'; stdout is user JSON; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.UnblockUserReturns = User{ID:'u1', Blocked:false}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users unblock u1'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'u1'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin users unblock is registered in the agent interface with method POST, path /users/:id/unblock, auth admin.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin users unblock' has method='POST', path='/users/:id/unblock', auth='admin'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin users unblock'); assert entry.method == 'POST'; assert entry.path == '/users/:id/unblock'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs list without --include-blocked calls ListOrgs with IncludeBlocked:false and prints the org array as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs list (no flags)
test_cases.expected: ListOrgs called with opts.IncludeBlocked==false; stdout is JSON array with org o1; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.ListOrgsReturns = [Org{ID:'o1'}]; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs list'; assert mock.CapturedOpts.IncludeBlocked == false; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert len(parsed) == 1; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs list --include-blocked calls ListOrgs with IncludeBlocked:true.
preconditions: mock SDK ListOrgs returns []*apikit.Organization with blocked org
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs list --include-blocked
test_cases.expected: ListOrgs called with opts.IncludeBlocked==true; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.ListOrgsReturns = [Org{ID:'o1', Blocked:true}]; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs list --include-blocked'; assert mock.CapturedOpts.IncludeBlocked == true; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs list is registered in the agent interface with method GET, path /orgs, auth admin, and flag --include-blocked.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin orgs list' has method='GET', path='/orgs', auth='admin', flag include-blocked with type bool and default false
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin orgs list'); assert entry.method == 'GET'; assert entry.path == '/orgs'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'; flag = find(entry.flags, name='include-blocked'); assert flag.type == 'bool'; assert flag.default == false

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs create with --name, --slug, --url calls CreateOrg with a non-nil URL pointer and prints the org as JSON.
preconditions: mock SDK CreateOrg returns &apikit.Organization{ID:'o1', Name:'Acme', Slug:'acme'}
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs create --name Acme --slug acme --url https://acme.com
test_cases.expected: CreateOrg called with {Name:'Acme', Slug:'acme', URL:ptr('https://acme.com')}; stdout is org JSON; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.CreateOrgReturns = Org{ID:'o1'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs create --name Acme --slug acme --url https://acme.com'; assert mock.CapturedReq.Name == 'Acme'; assert mock.CapturedReq.Slug == 'acme'; assert *mock.CapturedReq.URL == 'https://acme.com'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs create without --url sets the URL field to nil in the CreateOrgRequest.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs create --name Acme --slug acme (no --url)
test_cases.expected: CreateOrg called with URL==nil in request
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.CreateOrgReturns = Org{ID:'o1'}; invoke 'admin orgs create --name Acme --slug acme'; assert mock.CapturedReq.URL == nil

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs create is registered in the agent interface with method POST, path /orgs, auth admin, required flags name/slug and optional flag url.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin orgs create' has method='POST', path='/orgs', auth='admin'; flags include name (required), slug (required), url (not required)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin orgs create'); assert entry.method == 'POST'; assert entry.path == '/orgs'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'; nameFlag = find(entry.flags,'name'); assert nameFlag.required == true; slugFlag = find(entry.flags,'slug'); assert slugFlag.required == true; urlFlag = find(entry.flags,'url'); assert urlFlag.required == false

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs update <id> with --name and --url calls UpdateOrg with non-nil pointer fields and prints the org as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs update o1 --name NewName --url https://new.com
test_cases.expected: UpdateOrg called with id='o1', req.Name==ptr('NewName'), req.URL==ptr('https://new.com'); stdout is org JSON; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.UpdateOrgReturns = Org{ID:'o1', Name:'NewName'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs update o1 --name NewName --url https://new.com'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'o1'; assert *mock.CapturedReq.Name == 'NewName'; assert *mock.CapturedReq.URL == 'https://new.com'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs update with no flags emits a warnf warning to stderr, still calls UpdateOrg with nil fields, prints org JSON, and exits 0.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs update o1 (no --name, no --url)
test_cases.expected: stderr contains warning from warnf('no fields specified for update'); UpdateOrg called with both fields nil; stdout is org JSON; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.UpdateOrgReturns = Org{ID:'o1'}; capture stdout and stderr; invoke 'admin orgs update o1'; assert 'no fields specified for update' in stderr; assert mock.CapturedReq.Name == nil; assert mock.CapturedReq.URL == nil; assert mock.UpdateOrgCalled == true; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs update is registered in the agent interface with method PATCH, path /orgs/:id, auth admin, positional arg id, optional flags name and url.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin orgs update' has method='PATCH', path='/orgs/:id', auth='admin', args=[{name:'id'}], flags name and url both optional
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin orgs update'); assert entry.method == 'PATCH'; assert entry.path == '/orgs/:id'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'; nameFlag = find(entry.flags,'name'); assert nameFlag.required == false; urlFlag = find(entry.flags,'url'); assert urlFlag.required == false

test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.DeleteOrgReturns = nil; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs delete o1'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'o1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed == {}; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs delete is registered in the agent interface with method DELETE, path /orgs/:id, auth admin.

test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin orgs delete' has method='DELETE', path='/orgs/:id', auth='admin', args=[{name:'id'}]
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin orgs delete'); assert entry.method == 'DELETE'; assert entry.path == '/orgs/:id'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs block <id> calls BlockOrg and prints the returned org as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs block o1
test_cases.expected: BlockOrg called with id='o1'; stdout is org JSON; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.BlockOrgReturns = Org{ID:'o1', Blocked:true}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs block o1'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'o1'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs block is registered in the agent interface with method POST, path /orgs/:id/block, auth admin.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin orgs block' has method='POST', path='/orgs/:id/block', auth='admin'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin orgs block'); assert entry.method == 'POST'; assert entry.path == '/orgs/:id/block'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs unblock <id> calls UnblockOrg and prints the returned org as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs unblock o1
test_cases.expected: UnblockOrg called with id='o1'; stdout is org JSON; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.UnblockOrgReturns = Org{ID:'o1', Blocked:false}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs unblock o1'; assert mock.CapturedID == 'o1'; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs unblock is registered in the agent interface with method POST, path /orgs/:id/unblock, auth admin.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin orgs unblock' has method='POST', path='/orgs/:id/unblock', auth='admin'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin orgs unblock'); assert entry.method == 'POST'; assert entry.path == '/orgs/:id/unblock'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs members list <id> calls ListOrgMembers with the org id and prints the member array as JSON.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs members list o1
test_cases.expected: ListOrgMembers called with orgID='o1'; stdout is JSON array of users; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.ListOrgMembersReturns = [User{ID:'u1'}]; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs members list o1'; assert mock.CapturedOrgID == 'o1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert len(parsed) == 1; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs members list is registered in the agent interface with method GET, path /orgs/:id/members, auth admin.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin orgs members list' has method='GET', path='/orgs/:id/members', auth='admin', args=[{name:'id'}]
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin orgs members list'); assert entry.method == 'GET'; assert entry.path == '/orgs/:id/members'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs members add <org_id> <user_id> calls AddOrgMember and prints '{}' on success.
preconditions: mock SDK AddOrgMember returns nil error
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs members add o1 u1
test_cases.expected: AddOrgMember called with orgID='o1', userID='u1'; stdout contains '{}'; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.AddOrgMemberReturns = nil; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs members add o1 u1'; assert mock.CapturedOrgID == 'o1'; assert mock.CapturedUserID == 'u1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed == {}; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs members add is registered in the agent interface with method PUT, path /orgs/:id/members/:user_id, auth admin, two positional args.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: entry for 'admin orgs members add' has method='PUT', path='/orgs/:id/members/:user_id', auth='admin', args=[org_id, user_id]
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: entry = find(JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands, name='admin orgs members add'); assert entry.method == 'PUT'; assert entry.path == '/orgs/:id/members/:user_id'; assert entry.auth == 'admin'; argNames = [a.name for a in entry.args]; assert 'org_id' in argNames; assert 'user_id' in argNames

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs members remove <org_id> <user_id> calls RemoveOrgMember and prints '{}' on success.
preconditions: mock SDK RemoveOrgMember returns nil error
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs members remove o1 u1
test_cases.expected: RemoveOrgMember called with orgID='o1', userID='u1'; stdout contains '{}'; exit code 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.RemoveOrgMemberReturns = nil; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs members remove o1 u1'; assert mock.CapturedOrgID == 'o1'; assert mock.CapturedUserID == 'u1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed == {}; assert exitCode == 0

test_cases.description: akc admin orgs members remove is registered in the agent interface with method DELETE, path /orgs/:id/members/:user_id, auth admin.

test_cases.description: Every successful admin command writes valid JSON to stdout using json.MarshalIndent with two-space indentation.
preconditions: mock SDK returns a user object for admin users show
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users show u1
test_cases.expected: stdout is a valid JSON string; json.MarshalIndent with two-space indent is used (output is indented)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users show u1'; assert isValidJSON(stdout); assert '  ' in stdout // two-space indent present

test_cases.description: Every void-response command prints exactly the empty JSON object '{}' to stdout on success.
preconditions: mock SDK DeleteOrg returns nil; mock SDK RevokeUserKey returns nil
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs delete o1; separately invoke akc admin keys revoke u1 k1
test_cases.expected: stdout for each command parses as empty JSON object {}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for cmd in ['admin orgs delete o1', 'admin keys revoke u1 k1']: capture stdout; invoke cmd; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed == {} OR JSON.stringify(parsed) == '{}'

test_cases.description: Warning and informational messages go exclusively to stderr via warnf; stdout contains only JSON.
preconditions: mock SDK UpdateOrg returns org; no --name or --url provided
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin orgs update o1 with no flags
test_cases.expected: stdout is valid JSON; stderr contains the warnf warning; no warning text appears on stdout
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout and stderr; invoke 'admin orgs update o1'; assert isValidJSON(stdout); assert 'no fields specified for update' in stderr; assert 'no fields specified for update' not in stdout

test_cases.description: Exit codes are exactly 0 on success, 1 on APIError, and 2 on client error, with no other codes produced.
preconditions: three separate invocations: success, APIError, network error
test_cases.input: invoke commands in success, APIError, and network-error scenarios
test_cases.expected: exit code is 0 for success, 1 for APIError, 2 for network error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: assert exitCode('admin users list', mockSuccess) == 0; assert exitCode('admin users show u1', mockAPIError(404)) == 1; assert exitCode('admin users list', mockNetworkError) == 2

test_cases.description: All error output paths produce a valid JSON error envelope on stdout with code N for API errors and 0 for client errors.
preconditions: mock returns APIError{Code:404}; separate invocation with network error
test_cases.input: invoke admin users show with APIError 404; separately invoke with network error
test_cases.expected: both stdouts are valid JSON matching {"error":{"code":N,"message":"..."}}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout1 for APIError case; parsed1 = JSON.parse(stdout1); assert 'error' in parsed1; assert parsed1.error.code == 404; capture stdout2 for network error; parsed2 = JSON.parse(stdout2); assert parsed2.error.code == 0

test_cases.description: When the server returns HTTP 403, the CLI prints the 403 error envelope to stdout and exits with code 1.
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users list where SDK returns 403 APIError
test_cases.expected: stdout is {"error":{"code":403,"message":"forbidden"}}; exit code 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.ListUsersReturns = APIError{Code:403, Message:'forbidden'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users list'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.code == 403; assert parsed.error.message == 'forbidden'; assert exitCode == 1

test_cases.description: When the server returns HTTP 404, the CLI prints the 404 error envelope to stdout and exits with code 1.
preconditions: mock SDK returns APIError{Code:404, Message:'user not found'}
test_cases.input: invoke akc admin users show u999 where SDK returns 404 APIError
test_cases.expected: stdout is {"error":{"code":404,"message":"user not found"}}; exit code 1
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: mock.GetUserByIDReturns = APIError{Code:404, Message:'user not found'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users show u999'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.code == 404; assert exitCode == 1

test_cases.description: Every admin command appears in akc help --json output with auth set to 'admin'.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: all admin commands (admin users list, admin users show, admin users create, admin users update, admin users promote, admin users demote, admin users block, admin users unblock, admin orgs list, admin orgs create, admin orgs update, admin orgs delete, admin orgs block, admin orgs unblock, admin orgs members list, admin orgs members add, admin orgs members remove, admin keys list, admin keys revoke, admin tokens list, admin tokens revoke) each have auth=='admin'
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: cmds = JSON.parse(run('akc help --json')).commands; adminCmds = [c for c in cmds if c.name.startsWith('admin')]; for cmd in adminCmds: assert cmd.auth == 'admin'

test_cases.description: Each admin command's agent interface entry includes name, description, method, path, args array, and flags array with required fields populated.
test_cases.input: run akc help --json
test_cases.expected: each admin command entry has non-empty name, description, method, path; args is an array; flags is an array with name/type/required/default/description per flag
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: for entry in adminCmdsFromHelpJSON(): assert entry.name != ''; assert entry.description != ''; assert entry.method != ''; assert entry.path != ''; assert isArray(entry.args); assert isArray(entry.flags); for flag in entry.flags: assert flag.name != ''; assert flag.type != ''

test_cases.description: Each admin test file defines an unexported test-scoped interface and injects a mock into the command, with no production code export of the interface.
preconditions: Go test files admin_users_test.go, admin_orgs_test.go, admin_keys_test.go, admin_tokens_test.go exist
test_cases.input: run go test ./internal/cli/...
test_cases.expected: tests compile and pass; mock interfaces are unexported and defined only in _test.go files
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = run('go test ./internal/cli/...'); assert result.exitCode == 0; for file in test_files: interfaces = unexported_interfaces(file); assert len(interfaces) >= 1

test_cases.description: Each admin command test verifies correct SDK method, parameters, JSON stdout, exit code, and error envelope on validation failure.
preconditions: all mock SDK interfaces implemented in test files
test_cases.input: run go test ./internal/cli/... -v
test_cases.expected: all named test cases from the PRD Testing Strategy pass; go test exits 0
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: result = run('go test -v ./internal/cli/...'); assert result.exitCode == 0; assert 'PASS' in result.output

test_cases.description: No integration test files requiring a live or stubbed HTTP server are present in the repository layout for this spec.
test_cases.input: inspect internal/cli/ directory for integration test files
test_cases.expected: no file matches *integration_test.go or similar patterns within internal/cli/admin*.go scope
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: files = list_files('internal/cli/'); assert not any('integration' in f for f in files)

property_tests.description: For any admin command invocation (success or error), stdout always contains exactly one valid JSON value and nothing else.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random combinations of: (a) command name from the full admin command set, (b) valid or invalid positional args, (c) valid or invalid flags, (d) SDK mock returning success/APIError/networkError
property_tests.invariant_check: parse stdout as JSON; assert no parse error; assert stdout contains no non-JSON prefix or suffix text

property_tests.description: For any admin command invocation, the exit code is exactly 0, 1, or 2 — never any other value.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random combinations of command + args + flags + SDK response type (nil, APIError, networkError, missingArg scenario)
property_tests.invariant_check: exitCode = run(command, args, flags, mockSDK); assert exitCode in {0, 1, 2}

property_tests.description: For any file in internal/cli/admin*.go, no net/http client is constructed or used directly.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Static analysis over all files matching internal/cli/admin*.go
property_tests.invariant_check: for each file in glob('internal/cli/admin*.go'): ast = parseGo(file); assert 'net/http' not in imports(ast); assert no http.Client or http.Do calls in ast

property_tests.description: For any admin command invocation where a required positional argument or required flag is absent, no SDK method is ever called and exit code is 2.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For each command requiring positional args or required flags, generate invocations with each required item omitted one at a time
property_tests.invariant_check: mock.AnySdkMethodCalled = false; exitCode = run(command, missingArgOrFlag); assert mock.AnySdkMethodCalled == false; assert exitCode == 2

property_tests.description: For any successful invocation of a void-response command (orgs delete, orgs members add, orgs members remove, keys revoke, tokens revoke), stdout is exactly '{}' and exit code is 0.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For each of the five void-response commands, generate valid inputs with mock SDK returning nil error
property_tests.invariant_check: capture stdout; exitCode = run(voidCmd, validArgs); parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert JSON.stringify(parsed) == '{}'; assert exitCode == 0

property_tests.description: For every command registered under akc admin, the agent interface entry includes auth='admin' and non-empty method, path, args, flags fields.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Enumerate all commands registered under the admin tree via NewAdminCmd().Commands() recursively
property_tests.invariant_check: helpJSON = run('akc help --json'); for each adminCmd in enumerateAdminCommands(): entry = find(helpJSON.commands, name=adminCmd.fullName); assert entry.auth == 'admin'; assert entry.method != ''; assert entry.path != ''

property_tests.description: For any SDK method invocation in any admin command, the context argument is context.Background() with no deadline and no cancellation.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: For each admin command, generate a valid invocation with a capturing mock SDK that records the context
property_tests.invariant_check: for each adminCmd: mock = CapturingMock(); run(adminCmd, validArgs, mock); ctx = mock.CapturedContext; assert ctx != nil; deadline, hasDeadline = ctx.Deadline(); assert hasDeadline == false; assert ctx.Done() == nil

edge_case_tests.description: Invoking a parent-only command (e.g. 'admin', 'admin users') directly prints help text and exits 0 without calling loadConfig or the SDK.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin (no subcommand)
edge_case_tests.expected: help text is printed; exit code 0; loadConfig not called; no SDK call made
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: loadConfigCalled = false; capture stdout; invoke 'admin'; assert exitCode == 0; assert loadConfigCalled == false; assert len(stdout) > 0

edge_case_tests.description: If printJSON fails (e.g. stdout is closed), the command exits with code 2.
preconditions: printJSON is stubbed to return an error
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users list where printJSON returns error
edge_case_tests.expected: exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: printJSONStub = func(v) { return error('write failed') }; invoke 'admin users list'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.description: When an OS signal (SIGINT) is received while an SDK call is in progress, Cobra terminates the process without special cleanup.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users list with a blocking SDK mock then send SIGINT
edge_case_tests.expected: process terminates; exit code is signal-determined; no panic or deadlock
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: proc = start('admin users list', blockingMock); send(SIGINT, proc); exitCode = wait(proc); assert exitCode != 0 // signal-determined

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin users show invoked without the <id> argument exits with code 2 and prints the missing-argument error envelope.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users show (no args)
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":0,"message":"missing required argument: id"}}; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users show' args=[]; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.code == 0; assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required argument: id'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin users create without --username exits with code 2 and prints the missing-flag error envelope.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users create --email a@b.com --provider local --provider-id p1
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":0,"message":"missing required flag: --username"}}; exit code 2; SDK not called
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mock.CreateUserCalled = false; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users create --email a@b.com --provider local --provider-id p1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required flag: --username'; assert exitCode == 2; assert mock.CreateUserCalled == false

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin users create without --email exits with code 2 and prints the missing-flag error envelope.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users create --username bob --provider local --provider-id p1
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains missing-flag error for --email; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users create --username bob --provider local --provider-id p1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required flag: --email'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin users create without --provider exits with code 2 and prints the missing-flag error envelope.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users create --username bob --email b@x.com --provider-id p1
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains missing-flag error for --provider; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users create --username bob --email b@x.com --provider-id p1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required flag: --provider'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin users create without --provider-id exits with code 2 and prints the missing-flag error envelope.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users create --username bob --email b@x.com --provider local
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains missing-flag error for --provider-id; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users create --username bob --email b@x.com --provider local'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required flag: --provider-id'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users update --full-name Alice (no id arg)
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":0,"message":"missing required argument: id"}}; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users update --full-name Alice' args=[]; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required argument: id'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin users update without --full-name exits with code 2 and prints the missing-flag error envelope; SDK is not called.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users update u1 (no --full-name flag)
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":0,"message":"missing required flag: --full-name"}}; exit code 2; SDK not called
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mock.UpdateUserByIDCalled = false; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users update u1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required flag: --full-name'; assert exitCode == 2; assert mock.UpdateUserByIDCalled == false

edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users promote (no args)
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains missing-argument error for id; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users promote' args=[]; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required argument: id'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin users demote without the <id> argument exits with code 2 and prints the missing-argument error envelope.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users demote (no args)
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains missing-argument error for id; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users demote' args=[]; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required argument: id'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin users demote when the server returns 409 last-admin conflict prints the 409 error envelope and exits with code 1.
preconditions: mock SDK DemoteUser returns APIError{Code:409, Message:'cannot demote the last admin'}
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin users demote u1
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout is {"error":{"code":409,"message":"cannot demote the last admin"}}; exit code 1
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mock.DemoteUserReturns = APIError{Code:409, Message:'cannot demote the last admin'}; capture stdout; invoke 'admin users demote u1'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.code == 409; assert parsed.error.message == 'cannot demote the last admin'; assert exitCode == 1

edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains missing-argument error for id; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users block' args=[]; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required argument: id'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains missing-argument error for id; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin users unblock' args=[]; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required argument: id'; assert exitCode == 2

edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":0,"message":"missing required flag: --name"}}; exit code 2; SDK not called
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mock.CreateOrgCalled = false; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs create --slug acme'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required flag: --name'; assert exitCode == 2; assert mock.CreateOrgCalled == false

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin orgs create without --slug exits with code 2 and prints the missing-flag error envelope.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin orgs create --name Acme
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains {"error":{"code":0,"message":"missing required flag: --slug"}}; exit code 2; SDK not called
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: mock.CreateOrgCalled = false; capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs create --name Acme'; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required flag: --slug'; assert exitCode == 2; assert mock.CreateOrgCalled == false

edge_case_tests.description: akc admin orgs update without the <id> positional argument exits with code 2 and prints the missing-argument error envelope.
edge_case_tests.input: invoke akc admin orgs update --name NewName (no id arg)
edge_case_tests.expected: stdout contains missing-argument error for id; exit code 2
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode: capture stdout; invoke 'admin orgs update --name NewName' args=[]; parsed = JSON.parse(stdout); assert parsed.error.message == 'missing required argument: id'; assert exitCode == 2

smoke_tests.description: Successful akc admin users list: loadConfig reads config, newClient constructs client, ListUsers called with IncludeBlocked:false, JSON array printed to stdout, exits 0.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'akc admin users list' with a valid config file containing endpoint URL and API key; mock SDK returns a non-empty user list
expected_effects: stdout is a valid JSON array of user objects

smoke_tests.description: API error path: akc admin users show with non-existent id returns 404 APIError, handleError prints error envelope to stdout, exits 1.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'akc admin users show abc-nonexistent-id' with valid config; mock SDK returns *apikit.APIError{Code:404, Message:'user not found'}

smoke_tests.description: Client validation error: akc admin users create with missing --provider-id flag prints missing-flag error envelope to stdout, exits 2, SDK not called.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'akc admin users create --username alice --email alice@example.com --provider local' (omitting --provider-id)

smoke_tests.description: Void-response path: akc admin orgs delete calls DeleteOrg, receives nil error, prints '{}' to stdout, exits 0.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'akc admin orgs delete org-uuid-123' with valid config; mock SDK DeleteOrg returns nil

smoke_tests.description: Empty-patch warning path: akc admin orgs update with no flags emits warnf warning to stderr, calls UpdateOrg with nil fields, prints org JSON, exits 0.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'akc admin orgs update org-uuid-456' with no --name or --url flags; mock UpdateOrg returns Org{ID:'org-uuid-456'}
expected_effects: stderr contains 'no fields specified for update'

smoke_tests.description: Network failure path: akc admin keys list encounters TCP connection failure, handleError prints code-0 error envelope to stdout, exits 2.
smoke_tests.trigger: Run 'akc admin keys list user-uuid-789' with valid config; mock SDK returns a plain error (not APIError)
expected_effects: stderr may be empty or contain only plain-text warnings

smoke_tests.description: Full agent-discovery-then-create flow: agent parses akc help --json, constructs admin users create command, executes it, receives user JSON on stdout.
smoke_tests.trigger: First run 'akc help --json' and verify admin users create entry; then run 'akc admin users create --username bot --email bot@ci.example.com --provider local --provider-id bot-001'
expected_effects: akc help --json includes admin users create entry with method=POST, auth=admin, four required flags
CreateUser called with correct request struct
stdout is valid user JSON with username='bot'
